title: 10 medicinal constituents of Entada phaseoloides (fish bean)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Entada phaseoloides quercetin is a bioactive flavonoid compound derived from the seed of the Entada phaseoloides plant. It has been extensively studied for its various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Quercetin has been shown to exhibit significant activity against several diseases, including cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders, making it a potential therapeutic agent in traditional medicine.

Entada phaseoloides kaempferol is a bioactive flavonoid glycoside extracted from the seeds of this climbing plant native to tropical Asia. This compound has been extensively studied for its potential therapeutic applications, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Kaempferol has been reported to possess immunomodulatory effects, protecting against oxidative stress and exhibiting significant cytotoxicity towards certain cancer cell lines.

Entada phaseoloides gallic acid is a type of polyphenolic compound found in the seeds and pods of the plant. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Gallic acid has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacterial and fungal species, making it a promising natural agent for wound healing and infection prevention.

Entada phaseoloides ellagic acid is a polyphenolic compound derived from its seeds. This antioxidant-rich molecule has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. Ellagic acid has been found to inhibit the growth of various cancer cells, while also exhibiting protective effects against cardiovascular disease and neurodegenerative disorders. Its unique chemical structure makes it a valuable candidate for further research in the fields of medicine and nutrition.

Entada phaseoloides oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid compound isolated from the seeds of Entada phaseoloides, a climbing plant native to tropical Africa. It has been reported to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiproliferative effects. Oleanolic acid has also been shown to exhibit cytotoxic activity against several types of cancer cells, making it a promising compound for the development of new anticancer therapies.

Entada phaseoloides ursolic acid is a naturally occurring pentacyclic triterpenoid compound found in the seed extract of Entada phaseoloides. It has been shown to possess various bioactive properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Ursolic acid has also demonstrated potential in treating various diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disorders, and respiratory infections, making it a promising compound for further research and development.

Entada phaseoloides catechin is a type of flavonoid found in various parts of this plant species. Catechin is a well-known antioxidant and has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It has also been shown to exhibit protective effects against cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of catechin from Entada phaseoloides.

Entada phaseoloides chlorogenic acid is a phenolic compound isolated from the seeds of this plant. It has been found to possess various biological activities, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Studies have shown that chlorogenic acid can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative damage, making it a potential natural remedy for preventing chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. Additionally, it has been demonstrated to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, further highlighting its therapeutic potential.

Entada phaseoloides galloylquinic acid is a bioactive compound extracted from the seeds of Entada phaseoloides. It has been studied for its various pharmacological properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, it has shown potential in inhibiting cancer cell growth and inducing apoptosis, making it a promising natural compound for the development of novel therapeutic agents.

Entada phaseoloides quinic acid is a naturally occurring compound found in its seeds. This bioactive compound has been reported to possess various pharmacological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Quinic acid has also been shown to exhibit cytotoxicity against certain cancer cell lines, making it a promising candidate for the development of new therapeutics.

intro: Entada phaseoloides has active constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. The plant's constituents can improve lives by reducing pain and inflammation in arthritis patients, treating skin infections, and protecting against oxidative stress-related diseases. For instance, the alkaloids present in Entada phaseoloides have been shown to exhibit potent analgesic effects, making it a potential natural remedy for pain management.
intro_desc: Entada phaseoloides has active constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic effects. The constituents in Entada phaseoloides can help alleviate chronic pain and improve insulin sensitivity, ultimately reducing the risk of complications associated with diabetes. This can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals living with diabetes.
